headline: Person of Interest 2025: the longlist
body_text:
.

It was once frowned upon to lend customers money for the purpose of selling them things they won't need, can't use, and have no hope of ever being able to afford. We had less intelligence back then.

[Person of the Year: Jensen Huang — FT]

Winner of the inaugural Donald Trump Grease Prize.

[‘Monumental betrayal’: Fifa’s controversial World Cup ticket pricing strategy — FT]

A sump-pump operator in the dank basement of AI’s circulatory economy.

[Eight odd things in CoreWeave’s IPO prospectus — FTAV]

“ChatGPT can make mistakes. Check important info before adding its bullshit to your balance sheet. See Cookie Preferences to confirm you’re not doing the Theranos thing again.”

[Oracle’s astonishing jam-tomorrow OpenAI trade — FTAV]

Officially recognised as Britain’s worst player of Four-Dimensional Chess.

[The résumé inflation report — FTAV]

Prone to garbled and irrelevant output that fails to meet agreed standards, Karp is a voluble fabulist purportedly in charge of a state-spon
